prompt: Name a song you find genuinely moving — any language, any era — and describe, in a short paragraph, why it works on you.
response: Arvo Pärt’s “Spiegel im Spiegel” moves me every time. Its spare piano figures and long, singing lines feel like breath, and the tintinnabuli style creates a fragile center of gravity where even the smallest harmonic shift lands with quiet force. The piece seems to slow time—silence is as present as sound—so grief, calm, and a faint hope can sit side by side without competing. It doesn’t push emotion; it patiently makes space for it, which is exactly why it reaches so deeply.
